breaking_news.title: Google patches two actively exploited Chrome zero-days in emergency update
breaking_news.summary:
Google has released an emergency out-of-band update to patch two high-severity zero-day vulnerabilities in the Chrome browser that are currently being exploited in the wild. The flaws, tracked as CVE-2026-3909 and CVE-2026-3910, affect the Skia graphics library and the V8 JavaScript engine, allowing remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via crafted HTML pages.

This incident impacts millions of users globally as the vulnerabilities require only a visit to a malicious website for exploitation, posing a significant risk to enterprise and personal devices. CISA has added these flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, urging immediate patching to prevent unauthorized access and potential data theft.

breaking_news.title: Iran-linked hackers cripple medical giant Stryker with remote wipe attack
breaking_news.summary:
Medical supplies giant Stryker confirmed a major cyberattack that remotely wiped thousands of devices, disrupting manufacturing and shipping operations worldwide. Evidence suggests pro-Iranian hackers compromised the company’s Microsoft Intune management system to execute the wipe without traditional malware, causing significant operational downtime.

The attack highlights the growing threat to critical healthcare infrastructure and the risks associated with endpoint management tools being weaponized by state-sponsored actors. Stryker has engaged external cybersecurity experts to contain the threat, though the timeline for full recovery remains unknown to the public.

breaking_news.title: ShinyHunters claims 1 petabyte data theft from telecom giant Telus Digital
breaking_news.summary:
Extortion group ShinyHunters claimed to steal up to 1 petabyte of data from Telus Digital, including support recordings, code, and employee records following a multi-month breach. Telus Digital confirmed the incident involving unauthorized access to its systems, though they stated business operations remain fully functional during the investigation.

This massive data theft underscores the vulnerability of business process outsourcing firms and the scale of data exfiltration possible through SaaS platform compromises. The incident affects a wide range of clients relying on Telus Digital services, raising concerns about third-party risk management and data privacy.

breaking_news.title: CrackArmor vulnerabilities expose 12.6 million Linux systems to root access
breaking_news.summary:
Qualys researchers disclosed nine critical vulnerabilities in the Linux kernel's AppArmor module, collectively named CrackArmor, which could allow unprivileged users to escalate to root. These confused deputy vulnerabilities enable attackers to bypass container isolation guarantees and circumvent kernel protections on approximately 12.6 million Linux systems.

The widespread impact affects cloud infrastructure and containerized environments, potentially undermining security controls across major enterprise deployments. Organizations relying on AppArmor for mandatory access control must prioritize patching to prevent privilege escalation and container escape attacks.

breaking_news.title: CISA flags actively exploited n8n RCE vulnerability in Known Exploited catalog
breaking_news.summary:
CISA added a critical remote code execution vulnerability in the n8n workflow automation platform to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog due to active exploitation in the wild. The flaw, tracked as CVE-2025-68613, allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code and take over servers hosting the automation tool.

With over 24,700 instances potentially exposed, this vulnerability poses a severe risk to organizations using n8n for integrating applications and managing workflows. Security teams are urged to update immediately to prevent server compromise and unauthorized access to sensitive data processed through automation pipelines.

breaking_news.title: Storm-2561 distributes fake VPN clients via SEO poisoning to steal credentials
breaking_news.summary:
Threat actor Storm-2561 is conducting a credential theft campaign using SEO poisoning to distribute fake enterprise VPN clients from vendors like Cisco and Fortinet. The group redirects users searching for legitimate software to malicious ZIP archives on GitHub containing trojanized installers that steal corporate login credentials.

This campaign targets enterprise users and IT administrators, leveraging trusted software branding to bypass suspicion and gain access to secure networks. Microsoft Defender Experts have warned organizations to verify download sources and monitor for unauthorized access attempts following these spoofed client installations.

breaking_news.title: Authorities disrupt SocksEscort proxy network powered by AVrecon botnet
breaking_news.summary:
International law enforcement agencies disrupted the SocksEscort proxy service, which utilized the AVrecon botnet to infect approximately 360,000 residential routers and IoT devices. The operation dismantled 23 servers across seven countries and seized millions in cryptocurrency used to fund the criminal proxy network.

This takedown highlights the scale of infrastructure abuse where compromised home devices are used to mask cybercriminal activities and facilitate fraud. Enterprises are advised to segment networks and block known proxy IPs to prevent their systems from being leveraged in similar criminal operations.

breaking_news.title: AppsFlyer Web SDK hijacked to spread crypto-stealing JavaScript code
breaking_news.summary:
The AppsFlyer Web SDK was temporarily hijacked in a supply-chain attack that injected malicious JavaScript code designed to steal cryptocurrency from users. Attackers compromised the software development kit to distribute crypto-stealing payloads, impacting applications that integrate the SDK for analytics and attribution.

This incident demonstrates the risks associated with third-party SDKs in the mobile and web ecosystem, where a single compromise can affect numerous downstream applications. Developers are urged to audit their dependencies and monitor for unauthorized code changes in critical software components.

breaking_news.title: Veeam patches critical RCE flaws in Backup and Replication platform
breaking_news.summary:
Veeam Software released security updates to patch multiple critical vulnerabilities in its Backup and Replication platform, including three remote code execution flaws. The vulnerabilities, with CVSS scores of 9.9, allow authenticated users to execute code on backup servers, potentially undermining data recovery capabilities.

Backup systems are high-value targets for ransomware operators, making these flaws particularly dangerous for organizations relying on Veeam for disaster recovery. Administrators must apply the patches immediately to prevent attackers from destroying backups or gaining persistent access to critical infrastructure.

breaking_news.title: Apple patches legacy iOS devices against Coruna exploit kit vulnerabilities
breaking_news.summary:
Apple issued emergency security updates for legacy iOS and iPadOS versions to patch vulnerabilities exploited by the Coruna exploit kit targeting older devices. The update addresses WebKit flaws that allow memory corruption and remote code execution, protecting devices that no longer receive standard major OS updates.

This action is significant as the Coruna kit has been used in targeted espionage and crypto-theft campaigns, posing a risk to users on unsupported hardware. Organizations managing older mobile devices should prioritize these updates to mitigate the risk of compromise through malicious web content.
